section \<open>A Tail-Recursive, Stack-Based Ackermann's Function\<close>

theory Ackermann
  imports "HOL-Library.Multiset_Order" "HOL-Library.Product_Lexorder"
begin
steps.preview: (* Title: HOL/Examples/Ackermann.thy Author: Larry Paulson *) section \<open>A Tail-Recursive, St...

steps.command:
fun ack :: "[nat, nat] \<Rightarrow> nat"

where
    "ack 0 n             = Suc n"
  | "ack (Suc m) 0       = ack m 1"
  | "ack (Suc m) (Suc n) = ack m (ack (Suc m) n)"
steps.preview: fun ack :: "[nat, nat] \<Rightarrow> nat" where "ack 0 n = Suc n" | "ack (Suc m) 0 = ack m 1" | "...

steps.command:
function (domintros) ackloop :: "nat list \<Rightarrow> nat"

where
    "ackloop (n # 0 # l)         = ackloop (Suc n # l)"
  | "ackloop (0 # Suc m # l)     = ackloop (1 # m # l)"
  | "ackloop (Suc n # Suc m # l) = ackloop (n # Suc m # m # l)"
  | "ackloop [m] = m"
  | "ackloop [] =  0"
steps.preview: function (domintros) ackloop :: "nat list \<Rightarrow> nat" where "ackloop (n # 0 # l) = ackloop...

steps.command: lemma "ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)"
steps.preview: lemma "ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)"
subgoals: 1. ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)
1. \<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack 0 n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # 0 # l)
2. \<And>m n l. (\<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (ack (Suc m) n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # Suc m # l)
1. \<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack 0 n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # 0 # l)
2. \<And>m n l. (\<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (ack (Suc m) n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # Suc m # l)
1. \<And>m n l. (\<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (ack (Suc m) n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # Suc m # l)
1. \<And>m n l. (\<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (ack (Suc m) n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # Suc m # l)
1. \<And>m n l. (\<And>n l. ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (ack (Suc m) n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # Suc m # l)

steps.command: by (induction n arbitrary: l) (simp_all add: Suc)
steps.preview: by (induction n arbitrary: l) (simp_all add: Suc)

steps.command:
lemma ackloop_dom_longer:
  "ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)"
steps.preview: lemma ackloop_dom_longer: "ackloop_dom (ack m n # l) \<Longrightarrow> ackloop_dom (n # m # l)"

steps.command: by (induction m n arbitrary: l rule: ack.induct) auto
steps.preview: by (induction m n arbitrary: l rule: ack.induct) auto

steps.command:
fun acklist :: "nat list \<Rightarrow> nat"

where
    "acklist (n#m#l) = acklist (ack m n # l)"
  | "acklist [m] = m"
  | "acklist [] =  0"

steps.command:
fun ack_mset :: "nat list \<Rightarrow> (nat\<times>nat) multiset"

where
    "ack_mset [] = {#}"
  | "ack_mset [x] = {#}"
  | "ack_mset (z#y#l) = mset ((y,z) # map (\<lambda>x. (Suc x, 0)) l)"

function Ackloop :: "nat list \<Rightarrow> nat"

where
    "Ackloop (n # 0 # l)         = Ackloop (Suc n # l)"
  | "Ackloop (0 # Suc m # l)     = Ackloop (1 # m # l)"
  | "Ackloop (Suc n # Suc m # l) = Ackloop (n # Suc m # m # l)"
  | "Ackloop [m] = m"

fun term_vars :: "('a, 'b) term \<Rightarrow> 'b set" where
  "term_vars (Var x) = {x}" |
  "term_vars (Fun f ts) = \<Union>(set (map term_vars ts))"

  "perms = {f. bij f \<and> finite {x. f x \<noteq> x}}"

lemma permsI [Pure.intro]:

assumes "bij f" and "MOST x. f x = x"

shows "f \<in> perms"

lemma perms_imp_MOST_eq:
  "f \<in> perms \<Longrightarrow> MOST x. f x = x"

section \<open>Cantor's Theorem\<close>

theory Cantor
  imports Main
begin

steps.command: theorem Cantor: "\<nexists>f :: 'a \<Rightarrow> 'a set. \<forall>A. \<exists>x. A = f x"
steps.preview: theorem Cantor: "\<nexists>f :: 'a \<Rightarrow> 'a set. \<forall>A. \<exists>x. A = f x"

steps.command: from * obtain a where "?D = f a" by blast
steps.preview: from * obtain a where "?D = f a" by blast

steps.command: moreover have "a \<in> ?D \<longleftrightarrow> a \<notin> f a" by blast
steps.preview: moreover have "a \<in> ?D \<longleftrightarrow> a \<notin> f a" by blast

section \<open>Coherent Logic Problems\<close>

theory Coherent
imports Main
begin
steps.preview: (* Title: HOL/Examples/Coherent.thy Author: Stefan Berghofer, TU Muenchen Author: Marc Bezem, Ins...

steps.command:
lemma p1p2:

assumes "col a b c l \<and> col d e f m"

and "col b f g n \<and> col c e g o"

and "col b d h p \<and> col a e h q"

and "col c d i r \<and> col a f i s"

and "el n o \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "el p q \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "el s r \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "\<And>A. el A A \<Longrightarrow> pl g A \<Longrightarrow> pl h A \<Longrightarrow> pl i A \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "\<And>A B C D. col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> pl A D"

and "\<And>A B C D. col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> pl B D"

and "\<And>A B C D. col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> pl C D"

and "\<And>A B. pl A B \<Longrightarrow> ep A A"

and "\<And>A B. ep A B \<Longrightarrow> ep B A"

and "\<And>A B C. ep A B \<Longrightarrow> ep B C \<Longrightarrow> ep A C"

and "\<And>A B. pl A B \<Longrightarrow> el B B"

and "\<And>A B. el A B \<Longrightarrow> el B A"

and "\<And>A B C. el A B \<Longrightarrow> el B C \<Longrightarrow> el A C"

and "\<And>A B C. ep A B \<Longrightarrow> pl B C \<Longrightarrow> pl A C"

and "\<And>A B C. pl A B \<Longrightarrow> el B C \<Longrightarrow> pl A C"

and "\<And>A B C D E F G H I J K L M N O P Q.
           col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> col E F G H \<Longrightarrow> col B G I J \<Longrightarrow> col C F I K \<Longrightarrow>
           col B E L M \<Longrightarrow> col A F L N \<Longrightarrow> col C E O P \<Longrightarrow> col A G O Q \<Longrightarrow>
           (\<exists> R. col I L O R) \<or> pl A H \<or> pl B H \<or> pl C H \<or> pl E D \<or> pl F D \<or> pl G D"

and "\<And>A B C D. pl A B \<Longrightarrow> pl A C \<Longrightarrow> pl D B \<Longrightarrow> pl D C \<Longrightarrow> ep A D \<or> el B C"

and "\<And>A B. ep A A \<Longrightarrow> ep B B \<Longrightarrow> \<exists>C. pl A C \<and> pl B C"

shows goal using assms
steps.preview: lemma p1p2: assumes "col a b c l \<and> col d e f m" and "col b f g n \<and> col c e g o" and "co...

steps.command:
lemma p2p1:

assumes "col a b c l \<and> col d e f m"

and "col b f g n \<and> col c e g o"

and "col b d h p \<and> col a e h q"

and "col c d i r \<and> col a f i s"

and "pl a m \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "pl b m \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "pl c m \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "pl d l \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "pl e l \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "pl f l \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "\<And>A. pl g A \<Longrightarrow> pl h A \<Longrightarrow> pl i A \<Longrightarrow> goal"

and "\<And>A B C D. col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> pl A D"

and "\<And>A B C D. col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> pl B D"

and "\<And>A B C D. col A B C D \<Longrightarrow> pl C D"

and "\<And>A B. pl A B \<Longrightarrow> ep A A"

and "\<And>A B. ep A B \<Longrightarrow> ep B A"

section \<open>Example of Declaring an Oracle\<close>

theory Iff_Oracle
  imports Main
begin
steps.preview: (* Title: HOL/Examples/Iff_Oracle.thy Author: Lawrence C Paulson, Cambridge University Computer L...

steps.command:
oracle iff_oracle = \<open>
  let
    fun mk_iff 1 = Var (("P", 0), \<^typ>\<open>bool\<close>)
      | mk_iff n = HOLogic.mk_eq (Var (("P", 0), \<^typ>\<open>bool\<close>), mk_iff (n - 1));
  in
    fn (thy, n) =>
      if n > 0 andalso n mod 2 = 0
      then Thm.global_cterm_of thy (HOLogic.mk_Trueprop (mk_iff n))
      else raise Fail ("iff_oracle: " ^ string_of_int n)
  end
\<close>

theorem sqrt_prime_irrational:

fixes p :: nat

assumes "prime p"

shows "sqrt p \<notin> \<rat>"

where n: "n \<noteq> 0"

and sqrt_rat: "\<bar>sqrt p\<bar> = m / n"

and "coprime m n" by (rule Rats_abs_nat_div_natE)

steps.preview: from n and sqrt_rat have "m = \<bar>sqrt p\<bar> * n" by simp

steps.preview: also have "\<dots> * n\<^sup>2 = p * n\<^sup>2" by simp

steps.preview: have "p dvd m \<and> p dvd n"

steps.command: then obtain k where "m = p * k" ..
steps.preview: then obtain k where "m = p * k" ..
